title: 5 Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit
body_text:
5	Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit
5.1	Overview of Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit
If a cheque that your financial institution cashes or accepts on deposit is invalid, the drawee branch can dishonour and return the cheque to your financial institution. Examine cheques carefully to reduce the risk of cheques being dishonoured and returned.
While most Canadian or US dollar items are processed through the clearing, some deposit items must be sent on collection or processed on FX Notes Plus; or the customer can request the sender to provide an incoming wire instead. Some items are never acceptable, even on collection.
Important
: 	Don芒聙聶t accept photocopies of items. Photocopies made by the branch or a depositing customer aren芒聙聶t allowed under Payments Canada Rules.
Note:	CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement, prohibits the exchange of paper items that exceed $25 million. This limit can芒聙聶t be avoided by splitting the payment into two or more items on the same day.
Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can refer to the Cheques Part for detailed procedures on negotiating cheques and obtaining endorsements.
5.2	Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture
While most Canadian and US dollar items can be processed in Branch Capture, there are exceptions. The tables in this section list the items that can be processed in Branch Capture and those that can芒聙聶t. Additional information, considerations, and/or exceptions are provided where available. If items can芒聙聶t be processed in Branch Capture (e.g., foreign funds cheques), alternatives for processing the items are provided where possible (e.g., FX Notes Plus or sending the item on collection). In some cases, items shouldn芒聙聶t be accepted on deposit. You must also verify the acceptability of individual deposit items. See Section 5.4, Verifying Information on Cheques, for more information.
Important, note the following:
We don芒聙聶t recommend sending collection requests to countries where obtaining settlement is problematic due to prevalent postal fraud (e.g., African nations, Greece, Iran).
Items drawn on countries that are on Canadian or US sanctions lists aren芒聙聶t acceptable, even on collection.
芒聢聮
For Canadian domiciled financial institutions, contact Global Affairs Canada for general information about countries subject to Canadian economic sanctions. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
芒聢聮
For US domiciled financial institutions, contact the US Department of the Treasury to obtain a current list of OFAC sanctioned countries. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
If you accept a cheque drawn on a financial institution domiciled outside of Canada or the US, be aware that not all countries accept cheques negotiated outside of their country.
To obtain settlement for a cheque drawn on a financial institution domiciled in one country and payable in the currency of another country, the cheque must be sent on collection to the financial institution on which it芒聙聶s drawn. For example, a cheque payable in US dollars but drawn on a financial institution domiciled in Great Britain must be sent on collection to the British financial institution.
Items that can be processed in Branch Capture
Type
Currency
Additional Information
Cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants
Canadian dollar
Must be drawn on a Canadian financial institution or eligible entity (e.g., Canada Post, Western Union). Receiver General Warrants are drawn on the Government of Canada.
Note: Items can芒聙聶t contain wording like the following examples. See the 芒聙聹Items that can芒聙聶t be Processed in Branch Capture芒聙聺 table for processing options.
芒聙聹Collectible through芒聙聺
芒聙聹Acceptable through芒聙聺
芒聙聹Presented for payment at芒聙聺
芒聙聹For acceptance by芒聙聺
Canadian dollar items acceptable for deposit are defined in CPA Rule A1. These items must be payable on demand, must be drawn on a domestic branch of a CPA member or the Receiver General of Canada, and can芒聙聶t exceed $25 million. This limit can芒聙聶t be avoided by splitting the payment into two or more items on the same day.
Canada Savings and Canada Premium (MICR) Bonds
Canadian dollar
Deposit separately from other Canadian deposit items. See Section 9.5, Creating a Branch Capture Deposit for more information.
See the Redeeming or Transferring Bonds Part for procedures on redeeming bonds.
Ontario Savings Bonds
Canadian dollar
Deposit separately from other Canadian deposit items. See Section 9.5, Creating a Branch Capture Deposit for more information.
For procedures on redeeming Ontario Savings Bonds, go to:
https://www.ontariosavingsbonds.com/
American Express (Amex) Travelers cheques
Canadian and US dollar
Amex travelers cheques only.
See the 芒聙聹Items that can芒聙聶t be processed in Branch Capture芒聙聺 table for information on processing other types of travelers cheques.
US dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders drawn on a:
US financial institution
Canadian financial institution
Foreign financial institution that芒聙聶s domiciled in Canada
US dollar
Cheques issued by the US Treasury don芒聙聶t follow regular time constraints for returning items. These cheques can be returned by the US Government (e.g., for fraud) up to one year from the time of clearing and there is no recourse for your financial institution. See Section 4.4, Policy on Accepting US Treasury Cheques.
Items that can芒聙聶t be processed in Branch Capture
Type
Currency
Additional Information
Cheque, draft, or money order drawn on a financial institution domiciled outside of Canada
Canadian dollar
The following options are available:
Advise the customer to request a wire in place of the cheque. Funds sent by wire may reduce settlement delays and costs. Provide the customer with your financial institution芒聙聶s routing instructions. Routing instructions are available as forms for the most common currencies. See the Wires Part for more information. The customer provides the routing instructions to the sender.
Send the item to the drawee branch on collection. Minimum $300.00 (CAD equivalent) due to cost. See Chapter 19, Accepting Cheques on Collection, for more information.
Important
: Canadian dollar cheques drawn on a Bank of America branch in the US can芒聙聶t be accepted, even on collection.
芒聙聹Old Style芒聙聺 Canadian Savings Bonds (Series 1 to Series 31) without MICR
Canadian dollar
Must be sent to Central 1 via armoured car, courier, or registered mail.
Because these items are negotiable instruments, Central 1 recommends that you refer to your risk management policies and assess how to send these items to Central 1. See the Redeeming or Transferring Bonds Part.
Non-American Express (AMEX) Travelers cheques
Any currency
Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t accept non-Amex travelers cheques for processing. Contact Travelex directly for reimbursement procedures.
Domestic US Postal money order
US dollar
Don芒聙聶t accept. The phrase 芒聙聹Negotiable only in the US and Possessions芒聙聺 appears on the front and back of the item.
Cheques or drafts with wording like:
芒聙聹Collectible through芒聙聺
芒聙聹Acceptable through
芒聙聹Presented for payment at芒聙聺
芒聙聹For acceptance by芒聙聺
Any currency
The following options are available:
Advise the customer to request a wire in place of the cheque. Funds sent by wire may reduce settlement delays and costs. Provide the customer with your financial institution芒聙聶s routing instructions. Routing instructions are available as forms for the most common currencies. See the Wires Part for more information. The customer provides the routing instructions to the sender.
Send the item to the drawee branch on collection. Minimum $300.00 (CAD equivalent) due to cost. See Chapter 19, Accepting Cheques on Collection, for more information.
International Postal Money Orders
Any currency
Don芒聙聶t accept, even on collection. Canada Post won芒聙聶t provide reimbursement.
Other foreign currency cheques, drafts, or money orders not previously described
Any currency
The following options are available:
Process the item in FX Notes Plus (if it芒聙聶s an acceptable currency). See the FX Notes Plus Part for more information.
Advise the customer to request a wire in place of the cheque. Funds sent by wire may reduce settlement delays and costs. Provide the customer with your financial institution芒聙聶s routing instructions. Routing instructions are available as forms for the most common currencies. See the Wires Part for more information. The customer provides the routing instructions to the sender.
Send the item to the drawee branch on collection. Minimum $300.00 (CAD equivalent) due to cost. See Chapter 19, Accepting Cheques on Collection, for more information.
Related Information
CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement
5.3	Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Corporate Capture
While most Canadian and US dollar items can be processed in Corporate Capture, there are exceptions. The following table lists the items that can be processed by business customers in Corporate Capture. Additional information, considerations, and/or exceptions are provided where appropriate.
Items not listed are subject to Branch Capture processing rules. Those items must be taken to your financial institution for processing, or they may not be acceptable even on collection. See Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, for more information. Business Customers must also verify the acceptability of individual deposit items. Procedures for business customers are available in the Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Capture User Guide, Form 5932, or Corporate Capture 芒聙聯 Capture and Supervisor Guide, Form 5931.
Important
: 	Advise business customers that they can芒聙聶t process photocopies of items. Photocopies aren芒聙聶t allowed under Payments Canada Rules.
Note:	CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement, prohibits the exchange of paper items that exceed $25 million. This limit can芒聙聶t be avoided by splitting the payment into two or more items on the same day.
Items Business Customers Can Process in Corporate Capture
Type
Currency
Additional Information
Cheques, money orders, drafts, or Receiver General Warrants
Canadian dollar
Cheques, drafts, and money orders must be drawn on a Canadian financial institution or eligible entity (e.g., Canada Post, Western Union). Receiver General Warrants are drawn on the Government of Canada.
Canadian dollar items acceptable for deposit are defined in CPA Rule A1. These items must be payable on demand and must be drawn on a domestic branch of a CPA member or the Receiver General of Canada.
Note: Business customers can芒聙聶t process cheques or drafts with wording like the following examples. These items must be taken to your financial institution. See the table 芒聙聹Items that can芒聙聶t be processed in Branch Capture芒聙聺 in Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, for processing options.
Examples:

芒聙聹Acceptable through芒聙聺
芒聙聹Presented for payment at芒聙聺
芒聙聹For acceptance by芒聙聺
American Express (Amex) Travelers cheques
Canadian and US dollar
Amex travelers cheques only.
Business customers can芒聙聶t process travelers cheques from other issuers (e.g., Travelex) in Corporate Capture, these items must be taken to your financial institution. See the table 芒聙聹Items that Can芒聙聶t be Processed in Branch Capture芒聙聺 in Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, for processing options.
Note: Advise business customers on your policy for accepting non-AMEX travelers cheques.
US dollar cheques, drafts, and money orders drawn on a:
US financial institution
Canadian financial institution
Foreign financial institution that芒聙聶s domiciled in Canada
US dollar
Cheques issued by the US Treasury don芒聙聶t follow regular time constraints for returning items. Advise customers of your policy on processing US Treasury cheques. See Section 5.2, Determining if Items Are Acceptable for Deposit in Branch Capture, for more information.
Note: Business customers can芒聙聶t process cheques or drafts with wording like the following examples. These items must be taken to your financial institution.
Examples:
芒聙聹Collectible through芒聙聺
芒聙聹Acceptable through芒聙聺
芒聙聹Presented for payment at芒聙聺

5.4	Verifying Information on Cheques
To reduce the risk that a cheque is dishonoured and returned, examine each cheque that is presented for deposit to ensure that it芒聙聶s acceptable. If appropriate, hold funds on the customer芒聙聶s account until you芒聙聶ve verified:
The item has a MICR line.
The body (written) and figure (numeric) amounts match.
The item isn芒聙聶t post-dated, or stale-dated.
Note:	Receiver General Warrants aren芒聙聶t subject to the 6-month stale-date rule.
The drawer芒聙聶s signature is provided.
The drawer has initialled all changes.
The payee(s) has endorsed the cheque.
Note:	It芒聙聶s prudent to obtain the payee芒聙聶s endorsement on all cheques. However, CPA Rule A3, Endorsement and Identification of Paper Items, specifies that the payee芒聙聶s endorsement isn芒聙聶t mandatory when a cheque is deposited to a person芒聙聶s account where that person is the same person as the named and intended payee on the cheque.
Important
: 	The rules for endorsement vary for cheques drawn on foreign financial institutions (i.e., those domiciled in the US). The foreign financial institution may accept a 芒聙聹deposited to the account of芒聙聺 stamp, or it may require the payee to endorse the cheque with the payee芒聙聶s name exactly as shown on the face of the cheque and with the payee芒聙聶s financial institution account number. If the item isn芒聙聶t endorsed as required, the financial institution will return the item.
To avoid possible returns on cheques drawn on foreign financial institutions, including those domiciled in the US, the payee must endorse the cheque with the payee芒聙聶s name exactly as shown on the face of the cheque and with the payee芒聙聶s financial institution account number. See Section 4.3, Policy on Obtaining the Payee芒聙聶s Endorsement, for more information.
The item doesn芒聙聶t exceed $25 million.
Note:	CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement, prohibits the exchange of paper items that exceed $25 million. This limit can芒聙聶t be avoided by splitting the payment into two or more items on the same day. A payment of $25 million or more can be settled through a wire transfer.
Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can refer to the Cheques Part for detailed procedures on negotiating cheques and obtaining endorsements.
Related Information
CPA Rule A1, General Rules Pertaining to Items Acceptable for Clearing, for the Purpose of Clearing and Settlement
CPA Rule A3, Endorsement and Identification of Paper Items
5.5	Receiver General Warrants
Requirement to Cash Receiver General Warrants
Financial institutions must cash Receiver General Warrants up to a maximum of $1,750 for non-members. The Government of Canada indemnifies your financial institution up to $1,750, but only if you follow the identification and endorsement requirements of CPA Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Receiver General Warrants for Non-Customers. See Rule G8 on the Payments Canada website for details. Contact information for Payments Canada is provided in Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
Financial institutions can cash Receiver General Warrants over $1,750, but they aren芒聙聶t required to do so. If the warrant exceeds $1,750, reimbursement is paid up to a maximum of $1,750.
Note:	This is the current limit at time of publication.
Inspect Receiver General Warrants carefully and don芒聙聶t accept those suspected of being altered or counterfeit. CPA Rule G8 indemnifies financial institutions against fraudulent warrants, but not against warrants with material alterations. The Master Bond Program in BC and Ontario covers financial institutions for direct losses due to materially altered warrants. Financial institutions in the Atlantic region should contact CUMIS for coverage of losses due to materially altered Receiver General Warrants.
Verification and Collection Procedures for Receiver General Warrants
If there芒聙聶s any doubt as to the validity of a Receiver General Warrant, contact the Cheque Redemption Control Directorate (CRCD). See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources, for contact information.
To send a request to the CRCD by fax, complete the Collection Item form provided in CPA Rule G7, Verification and Collection Procedures for Receiver General Warrants.
If the CRCD can芒聙聶t verify that the cheque is outstanding, send it on collection to the CRCD. Complete the Collection Item form provided in CPA Rule G7, Verification and Collection Procedures for Receiver General Warrants. Forward the completed form and the cheque to the CRCD address provided on the form.
Stale-Dated Receiver General Warrants
Receiver General Warrants aren芒聙聶t subject to the 6-month stale-date rule. Warrants dated six months or more before the date of presentation can be verified with or sent for collection to the CRCD.
Reimbursement of Receiver General Warrants
BC credit unions can refer to the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part for procedures on requesting reimbursement of Receiver General Warrants.
Related Information
CPA Rule G7, Verification and Collection Procedures for Receiver General Warrants
CPA Rule G8, Procedures/Criteria Pertaining to the Encashment of Government of Receiver General Warrants for Non-Customers
5.6	BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
This section applies to BC financial institutions only.
BC Employment and Assistance Cheques are issued by the BC government as a form of income assistance. BC Employment and Assistance Imprest Cheques are normally issued to replace lost or stolen cheques or to replace rejected direct deposit payments.
Financial institutions that participate in the BC Employment and Assistance Program (formerly known as the BC Benefits Program) must cash BC Employment and Assistance Cheques for members and non-members under the following circumstances:
the payee endorses the cheque in the presence of the teller,
the payee presents acceptable identification as defined by the provincial government, and
the amount of the cheque doesn芒聙聶t exceed the maximum face amount.
Note: 	The maximum face values are currently $4,500 for regular monthly BC Employment and Assistance Cheques, $3,000 for Imprest cheques issued at Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction Offices, and $3,000 for Imprest cheques issued at Ministry of Children and Family Development Offices.
Note:	These are the current limits at time of publication.
The provincial government will indemnify financial institutions for fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheques if the government芒聙聶s encashment procedures and identification requirements are followed. Fraudulent BC Employment and Assistance Cheques include counterfeit cheques, unauthorized material alterations to the face amount or payee, forged endorsements, and a forged drawer芒聙聶s signature.
Enrolling in the BC Employment and Assistance Program
Central 1 administers financial institution participation in the BC Employment and Assistance Program. This program is governed by the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366. Proceed as follows to participate in the program:
Review the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366, and print a copy for your records. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366, Page 1
Complete the Execution Document for Institutions, Form 3367. See Figure聽2.
Figure 2: Execution Document for Institutions, Form 3367
Make three copies of the Execution Document for Institutions, Form 3367, and ensure that the appropriate officers/directors sign all copies.
Forward two copies of the Execution Document for Institutions, Form 3367, to
Central 1
Payments & DBPX (Digital Banking Platforms & Experiences)
Associate Vice-President, Payment and Client Support Services
Central 1 will retain one copy and forward the other to the provincial government.
Retain one copy for your records.
Negotiating and Verifying BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
Financial institutions that participate in the BC Employment and Assistance Program must cash BC Employment and Assistance Cheques for members and non-members.
Ensure that:
The payee presents acceptable identification. If the payee is a member, identify the member according to your regular cheque cashing procedures. If the payee isn芒聙聶t a member, see the
Identification Requirements
on the Government of British Columbia website.
Note:	BC Services Cards are considered acceptable identification even though they aren芒聙聶t specifically listed in Schedule A, Acceptable Identification. See Schedule A, Acceptable Identification, in the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366.
Note:	It isn芒聙聶t mandatory for the address on the cheque to match the address on the identification.
Non-member payees who don芒聙聶t have acceptable identification, must visit a Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction (SDPR) office and ask for acceptable identification to be sent to their preferred financial institution. The Ministry may provide your financial institution with a special photo ID card. See Figure 3.
Note:	Photo ID cards issued by the Ministry are delivered directly to the branch by an SDPR staff member or by courier. Don芒聙聶t accept a Photo ID card from the person who presents the cheque.
Figure 3: Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction Photo ID Card
An employee of your financial institution is present while the payee endorses the cheque, and records on the back of the cheque the account number if the payee is a member or the identification number(s) if the payee isn芒聙聶t a member. See Schedule B, Encashment Procedures, in the BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366.
The cheque doesn芒聙聶t exceed the maximum face value. For regular monthly BC Employment and Assistance Cheques and Imprest cheques, the maximum face values are currently:
芒聢聮
$4,500 for regular monthly BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
芒聢聮
$3,000 for Imprest cheques issued by the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction
芒聢聮
$3,000 for Imprest cheques issued by the Ministry of Children and Family Development
Note:	These are the current limits at time of publication.
To combat fraudulent cheque activity, financial institutions can contact the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction to validate a BC Employment and Assistance Cheque or an Imprest cheque.
Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction
Phone:
778-405-4306
or
1-877-377-7702
Note:	Only call the Ministry if there are specific reasons to be concerned about the validity of a cheque. The BC Benefits Cheques Indemnity Agreement, Form 3366, doesn芒聙聶t require phone verification of all cheques.
As an additional security practice, the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction changes the colour of BC Employment and Assistance Cheques monthly. Financial institutions that process BC Employment and Assistance Cheques must be aware of the cheque colour of a given month. The Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction also sends out advisories each month with the following information to help you verify the validity of BC Employment and Assistance Cheques:
Cheque colour
Bank accounts
Financial institution number
Valid cheque number ranges
Note:	Imprest cheques don芒聙聶t vary from month to month.
Exemptions to Cheque Cashing Obligations
Your financial institution isn芒聙聶t required to cash BC Employment and Assistance cheques if
the person offering the cheque does not, at the same time, endorse the cheque or present adequate identification;
the person offering the cheque is grossly belligerent or obnoxious; or
you reasonably believe that the cheque is fraudulent, or the identification presented isn芒聙聶t genuine.
Reimbursement of BC Employment and Assistance Cheques
BC financial institutions can refer to the Dishonour and Return of Cheques Part for procedures on requesting reimbursement of BC Employment and Assistance Cheques.
Note:	Cheques returned for the reason 芒聙聹Payment Stopped芒聙聺 aren芒聙聶t classified as 芒聙聹fraudulent cheques芒聙聺 and consequently aren芒聙聶t specifically covered by the indemnity agreement. However, the province may honour reimbursement claims for these items if your financial institution states 芒聙聹holder in due course芒聙聺 as the reason for the claim. Process the claim in accordance with the indemnity agreement but note that there is no guarantee of reimbursement.
Related Information

Form 3366

Form 3367
